title: rubus atrebatum
intro: Rubus atrebatum is a species of wild raspberry native to the United Kingdom and Europe. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Rubus atrebatum include its ability to support cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and promote antioxidant activity in the body. Herbal preparations of Rubus atrebatum include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to treat skin conditions and internally to support overall health.
